title: Claim
description: A single falsifiable assertion extracted from a paper. The unit of structured knowledge in the rrxiv corpus. Claim graph edges (depends_on, supports, contradicts, extends) connect claims across papers.

id.description: Stable, globally unique claim ID. Once assigned, NEVER changes. Suggested format: <paper_id>:<local_label>.

paper_id.description: ID of the paper this claim originates in. Same paper may contain many claims.

statement.description: The claim itself, expressed as a single falsifiable assertion in natural language. Should be standalone-readable.

claim_type.description: What kind of claim this is.

evidence_type.description: What kind of evidence supports the claim within the paper.

scope.description: Conditions under which the claim is asserted to hold.

confidence.description: Author's stated confidence in the claim. Optional but encouraged.

depends_on.description: IDs of claims (in this or other papers) that this claim relies on. Forms the dependency edges of the claim graph.

supports.description: IDs of claims that this claim is presented as evidence for.

contradicts.description: IDs of claims this claim contradicts. Author asserts the contradiction; community may dispute.

extends.description: IDs of claims this claim extends or generalises.

replication_status.description: Aggregate replication status. Updated by server based on annotations.

source_location.description: Where in the source the claim originates, for provenance.

extracted_by.description: Who extracted this claim. Author-extracted claims are canonical from submission; others require confirmation.

canonical.description: True if confirmed by paper author or via quorum of credentialed annotators.

examples.statement: A protocol that exposes claims as first-class structured entities is queryable by agents in ways an unstructured PDF corpus is not.
